topStories.headline: Original Joe's Hit by Kitchen Fire; No Injuries Reported
topStories.dek: San Francisco firefighters quickly contained a one-alarm fire at the flagship North Beach restaurant Wednesday evening, marking the owners' third fire in two decades.
topStories.body:
San Francisco firefighters responded to Original Joe's on Wednesday evening after heavy smoke was seen rising from the roof of the landmark North Beach restaurant at Union and Stockton streets. The San Francisco Fire Department reported that the fire, which started in a chimney or exhaust flue coming out of the kitchen, was contained by 8:19 p.m.

Part of Union Street and a section of Stockton Street were temporarily closed for the firefight and cleanup. The restaurant and a neighboring building were both evacuated during the response. The SFFD reported no injuries or displacements as of 9:11 p.m., and the building was turned back over to its owners that night.

The incident marks the third fire connected to Original Joe's ownership in 20 years. The flagship North Beach location, which opened in this space in 2012, moved there after its original Tenderloin location burned down. As of Thursday, it remained unclear whether the restaurant suffered serious damage. Original Joe's had not posted any update to its social media.

Just two days earlier, on Monday night, firefighters had also extinguished a separate small fire on the roof of a building on the 400 block of Green Street in North Beach. That fire broke out around 8:36 p.m. and spread to some interior walls before being put out about 20 minutes later. No one was displaced in that incident either.

topStories.headline: Verdi Building Demolition Appeal Heads to Board of Appeals
topStories.dek: The contentious battle over the fire-gutted Verdi Building at 659 Union Street nears a pivotal hearing, as preservationists and the developer clash over the future of the historic North Beach site.
topStories.body:
The long-running dispute over the burned-out Verdi Building at 659 Union Street is heading toward a decisive moment. An appeal by the North Beach Tenants Committee has halted demolition of the century-old structure, which has sat empty since a 2018 fire gutted its interior. A hearing before the San Francisco Board of Appeals is expected later this month.

Developer Red Bridge Partners, led by Jeff Jurow, obtained an emergency permit in March to demolish the structure after two engineering firms identified it as an immediate collapse hazard. But just as excavators were about to begin, the North Beach Tenants Committee appealed, arguing that the city approved a full teardown under a permit that described only a 50-foot section of rear wall removal.

The Telegraph Hill Dwellers and other preservation groups have also weighed in, contending the Verdi Building is eligible for listing on national and state historic registers and that demolition would require a thorough environmental review. They have also raised concerns that the proposed replacement — an eight-story building with 89 apartments, a rooftop restaurant, and ground-floor retail — could cast shadows on Washington Square Park in violation of Proposition K.

District 3 Supervisor Danny Sauter has supported the demolition on safety grounds, stating that the property has been deteriorating for more than eight years. The Port of San Francisco's Fisherman's Wharf Forward survey — due today, May 22 — is a separate but concurrent effort shaping the broader waterfront conversation.

topStories.headline: Fisherman's Wharf Forward: New Plaza and Lagoon Overlook on Track for Summer
topStories.dek: The Port of San Francisco's $10 million revitalization of Fisherman's Wharf nears completion of its near-term phase, with a new public plaza and inner lagoon overlook expected to open this summer — just in time for the World Cup.
topStories.body:
The Port of San Francisco's Fisherman's Wharf Forward project is entering a critical stretch, with near-term enhancements scheduled for completion by this summer. A new 5,000-square-foot public plaza on Taylor Street — on the site of the former Alioto's Restaurant — will feature wave-shaped seating, picnic tables, and views of the Golden Gate Bridge and the Inner Lagoon's fishing fleet.

The project also includes a new inner lagoon overlook along Al Scoma Way and upgraded lighting to celebrate the historic fishing vessels. The upgrades are part of a $10 million initiative aimed at revitalizing Taylor Street, Jefferson Street, and the Inner Lagoon, reconnecting visitors to the working waterfront ahead of San Francisco's role as a 2026 FIFA World Cup host city.

The long-term phase of the project, focused on seismic and flood resilience improvements, will continue through fall 2030. The Port held a community meeting on April 30 and is accepting public survey responses through Friday, May 22 to help shape the conceptual plan. Some community members have launched an effort to name the new plaza after the Alioto family, which first opened their fish stand on the wharf 101 years ago.

Meanwhile, fishermen have raised concerns about the balance between tourism and the working waterfront. Industry leaders have noted aging infrastructure, including outdated ice-making equipment, and the loss of the wharf's longtime machine shop and boatworking facilities.

events.title: Memorial Day Lost Boat Ceremony aboard USS Pampanito
events.description: The San Francisco Maritime National Park Association hosts a free Memorial Day ceremony honoring submariners who made the ultimate sacrifice. The event includes a solemn roll call of submarines on 'eternal patrol,' the tolling of a muted bell, and a ceremonial flag dip.
events.sourceName: David Perry & Associates / SF Maritime National Park Association

events.title: SF City Guides: Free Memorial Day Weekend Walking Tours
events.location: Various locations including North Beach (meet at Saints Peter & Paul Church, 666 Filbert St.)
events.description: San Francisco City Guides offers 50+ free walking tours over Memorial Day weekend, including the popular North Beach Italian Heritage tour. Free and open to the public; donations welcome.

events.title: 'Dear San Francisco' at Club Fugazi
events.location: Club Fugazi, 678 Green Street, North Beach
events.description: The critically acclaimed acrobatic love letter to San Francisco by The 7 Fingers continues its residency in the heart of North Beach. The intimate, immersive 90-minute circus show features world-class acrobats. Tickets from $59.

events.title: Festa Italiana Street Fair
events.description: San Francisco's only Italian street festival returns with Italian food, wine, the famous pizza toss by Tony Gemignani, tarantella dancing, live Italian music, a symphony of accordions, and the 105th Statuto Race on Sunday morning. Free admission.

events.title: North Beach Festival 2026
events.location: Grant Avenue, Columbus Avenue & surrounding streets, North Beach
events.description: The 72nd annual North Beach Festival spans 11 blocks with 200+ vendors, beer gardens, live music on three stages, Italian street painting, the blessing of the animals, and a kids' chalk art area. Free admission.

events.title: Fisherman's Wharf Forward Community Survey Deadline
events.description: Today is the last day to respond to the Port of San Francisco's public survey helping shape the long-term vision for the Fisherman's Wharf Forward project, including seismic and flood resilience improvements.

moreNews.headline: Buena Vista Bartender Joseph Shaw Celebrates 50 Years of Pouring Irish Coffees
moreNews.body: Joseph Shaw, the longest-serving bartender at The Buena Vista on Hyde and Beach streets, was honored on May 15 for 50 years behind the bar. Shaw, who started as a barback in 1976, marked the occasion by pouring a ceremonial row of 50 Irish coffees for a crowd of regulars, tourists, and former colleagues. The San Francisco Board of Supervisors officially recognized Shaw's dedication with a resolution and certificate of honor. At 72 years old, Shaw plans to continue working at the bar while transitioning to part-time hours.

moreNews.headline: Fisherman's Wharf Hits 1 Million Monthly Visitors for First Time Since Pandemic
moreNews.body: Fisherman's Wharf recorded one million visitors in a single month for the first time since the pandemic, according to the San Francisco Examiner — a 24% increase over the same period in 2024. The milestone comes as the neighborhood undergoes its Fisherman's Wharf Forward makeover and prepares for the summer World Cup tourist surge. Bri Maughan, executive director of the Fisherman's Wharf Community Benefit District, called the wharf a dynamic, evolving neighborhood with a clear commitment to maintaining it as a world-class destination.

moreNews.headline: Two North Beach Building Fires in One Week Prompt Safety Concerns
moreNews.body: North Beach experienced two separate building fires within three days this week. On Monday, May 18, firefighters extinguished a roof fire at a mixed-use building on the 400 block of Green Street, containing both residential and commercial units. No one was displaced. Then on Wednesday, May 20, the kitchen flue fire at Original Joe's prompted a large fire department response at Union and Stockton streets. While neither fire resulted in injuries, the back-to-back incidents have heightened awareness of fire safety in the dense, historic neighborhood.
